mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recessionary pressures, which systematically depress S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE breaches 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates stagflationary pressure, forcing rapid equity repricing and generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns when the economy operates above potential output.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 45% of expansionary INDPRO periods, or the mechanism reverses direction during supply-constrained environments.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which systematically supports positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC drops below 0.01 over any 24-month window, or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive quarterly equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which immediately compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in over 55% of months following inflation prints above 0.5%, or the threshold loses predictive power after 2023.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with accelerating gross domestic product signals synchronized demand expansion across residential and aggregate sectors, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production confirm a manufacturing contraction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Short-term equity returns remain positive in over 60% of double-dip INDPRO months, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.
